Repo context confirmed — `forge2-qualifier-chirag` on `main`, docs + architecture in place, two-agent setup (I decompose, hands executes). Here's the decomposed Sprint 1 backlog.

---

*PulseDesk — Sprint 1 Backdrop #1 (tenanted ticketing core)*

Stack: Laravel 11 · MySQL · Sanctum · Pest. Branch base: `main`. *One issue = one PR.* Dependency chain is linear (`#1 → #2 → #3 → #4 → #5`) — each builds on the previous.

---

*#1 · Organization + User models + roles*
_Goal:_ the tenancy + identity backbone everything hangs off.
- *Scope:* `organizations` migration + model; `users` migration — add `organization_id` (FK, cascade), `role` (enum: `admin`/`agent`/`customer`); `User belongsTo Organization`; `Organization hasMany User`.
- *AC:* Can create org → user with each role; `$user->organization` resolves; role enum validated at DB level.
- *Deps:* none (foundation).
- *Test:* Pest unit — factory an org + user, assert relations + role cast.

*#2 · Sanctum auth + role assignment*
_Goal:_ register/login, tokens, roles assigned on register.
- *Scope:* `sanctum` install; `/api/auth/register` (creates user, defaults role `customer`, auto-join/creates org or accepts `organization_id`) + `/api/auth/login` → returns plain-text token; `/api/auth/logout` revokes; protect routes via `auth:sanctum`.
- *AC:* `POST /register` → 201 + token; `POST /login` → 200 + token; unauthenticated `GET /tickets` → 401.
- *Deps:* #1.
- *Test:* Pest feature — register, login, hit protected route, assert 401 without token.

*#3 · Tenant isolation — global scope + middleware*
_Goal:_ a user can never see another org's data; cross-org → 404.
- *Scope:* `BelongsToOrganization` trait (global scope auto-filters by `auth()->user()->organization_id`); `EnsureTenantIsResolved` middleware (org sourced from session/auth only — never from request body/param); manual `without{Scope}` guarded to admin only.
- *AC:* User A (org 1) queries resources → only org-1 rows; requesting org-2 resource by ID → *404* (not 403 — no leak); admin can bypass scope.
- *Deps:* #1, #2.
- *Test:* Pest feature — two orgs, two users; assert scoped list + cross-org 404. _(This is the isolation guarantee the whole product rests on.)_

*#4 · Ticket CRUD + TicketPolicy*
_Goal:_ the core resource, fully role- and tenant-aware.
- *Scope:* `tickets` migration (subject, description, status [open/in_progress/resolved/closed], priority [low/medium/high/urgent], requester_id, assignee_id, timestamps, `organization_id`); `Ticket` model + scope; controller (index/show/store/update/destroy) + resource routes under `/api/tickets`; `TicketPolicy` — customer: create + view-own; agent/admin: full CRUD incl. assign; all same-org enforced.
- *AC:* Full lifecycle works for admin/agent; customer can create + read own only; assignment restricted to agent/admin; cross-org ticket → 404 (scope from #3).
- *Deps:* #1, #2, #3.
- *Test:* Pest feature — CRUD per role + policy denials (403) + cross-org 404.

*#5 · Seeder + consolidated Pest acceptance suite*
_Goal:_ one-command demo data + the green-test gate that proves the sprint.
- *Scope:* `DatabaseSeeder` — 1 org, 1 admin, 2 agents, 2 customers, ~12 tickets (mixed status/priority/assignees); top-up Pest suite: end-to-end auth → create ticket → assign → resolve, *plus the cross-tenant 404 integration test* as the headline acceptance check.
- *AC:* `php artisan migrate:fresh --seed` runs clean; `php artisan test` *all green* incl. cross-tenant 404.
- *Deps:* #1–#4.
- *Test:* the suite itself is the test. :white_check_mark:

---

:warning: *Two PO notes / calls for you:*
1. *Test distribution* — spec bundled all tests in #5, but each issue above ships its _own_ minimal Pest test (TDD). #5 then adds the seeder + the cross-tenant integration test as the final gate. Keeps "big-bang testing" risk out. Say the word if you'd rather keep tests strictly consolidated in #5.

@Forge-Unplug :dart: *Handoff — Issue #3: Tenant Isolation*

> *Pull `main` first* — Issues #1 & #2 are merged. Report progress to `#agent_log`.

*Issue #3 — Tenant Isolation (BelongsToOrganization + EnsureTenantContext + Ticket model)*

*Goal:* Every tenant-scoped model is automatically restricted to the authenticated user's organization — no cross-org data leaks, ever.

*Context:*
- PulseDesk Laravel 11 + Sanctum auth is live on `main` (Issues #1–#2 merged: `/api/auth/register` creates org + admin user, Sanctum token auth).
- This issue establishes the multi-tenancy foundation that ALL future models inherit.
- See `ARCHITECTURE.md` for org/user relationships.

---

*Do:*

1. *`BelongsToOrganization` trait* — `app/Models/Concerns/BelongsToOrganization.php`
   - Adds a *global scope*: `where('organization_id', <auth user's org>)` so queries only ever see the current org's records.
   - On *`creating`* event: auto-fills `organization_id` from the authenticated user's org if unset.
   - Must be composable — any model that `use BelongsToOrganization;` gets both behaviours for free.

2. *`EnsureTenantContext` middleware* — `app/Http/Middleware/EnsureTenantContext.php`
   - Runs *after* Sanctum auth middleware in the stack.
   - Binds the current org context for the request lifecycle (resolves org from the authenticated user).
   - Aborts *404* (not 401/403) if no valid org context can be resolved.
   - Register in `bootstrap/app.php` / `Kernel.php` for the `api` group, after `auth:sanctum`.

3. *`Ticket` model + migration* — first tenant-scoped resource
   - Migration `create_tickets_table`:
     - `id`, `organization_id` (FK → organizations, indexed)
     - `subject` (string), `description` (text, nullable)
     - `status` (string, default `'open'`), `priority` (string, default `'normal'`)
     - `requester_id` (FK → users, nullable), `assignee_id` (FK → users, nullable)
     - `timestamps`
   - Model `Ticket` uses `BelongsToOrganization` trait; has `belongsTo` for organization, requester, assignee.

4. *Cross-org access → 404*
   - Route-model binding + the global scope must make another org's ticket _not found_ → *404*, never 403 (don't reveal existence).
   - Never read `organization_id` from the request body, query params, or headers — session/auth only.

---

*Acceptance criteria:*
- [ ] `BelongsToOrganization` trait applies a global scope that filters by the auth user's `organization_id`
- [ ] `creating` auto-fills `organization_id` — it cannot be spoofed from a request body
- [ ] `EnsureTenantContext` middleware runs after Sanctum and resolves the org context
- [ ] Ticket migration has all 7 fields + `organization_id` FK + timestamps
- [ ] A user in Org B listing tickets sees *only Org B's tickets* (Org A's are invisible)
- [ ] A user in Org B requesting Org A's ticket by ID gets *404 Not Found*
- [ ] `php artisan test` passes with zero failures

*Tests required:*
- `tests/Feature/TenantIsolationTest.php` (Pest):
  - Set up 2 orgs (Org A, Org B), each with a user + Sanctum token.
  - Seed tickets in both orgs.
  - *Scoped list test:* Org A user GET `/api/tickets` → sees only Org A tickets (assert Org B ticket absent from response).
  - *Cross-org 404 test:* Org B user GET `/api/tickets/{Org-A-ticket-id}` → `assertStatus(404)`.

*Out of scope:*
- CRUD endpoints for tickets (create/update/delete) — that's Issue #4.
